experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the upper-middle, currently vertical (rotation 1). The stack below has a relatively flat center with gaps and a left-side jagged area; there is no immediate line clear available this turn. Goal is to place the Z efficiently without creating overhangs, and since next pieces include I and O, preserving a clean central well is useful. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right would switch to horizontal and could set up a flatter placement, but first moving left is better because the left side has a contour where a horizontal Z can likely fit; moving right would drift away from the main fitting area, and dropping now would commit the awkward vertical placement high above the stack. So the best immediate sub-goal is to shift left before later rotating/dropping into the left-center contour.
